items.title: Broken object level authorization (BOLA/IDOR)
items.description: Swap object IDs across users and roles; test horizontal and vertical access. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Authentication and Authorization. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: Authentication and Authorization: Swap object IDs across users and roles; test horizontal and vertical access
items.how_to_fix: Enforce object-level authorization on every endpoint using server-side ownership checks - never trust client-supplied IDs alone.

items.title: JWT validation flaws
items.description: alg:none, weak secrets, missing exp/aud, and key confusion attacks. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Authentication and Authorization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Authentication and Authorization: Test "JWT validation flaws" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Validate alg, iss, aud, and exp; use short-lived tokens; rotate signing keys; reject `none` and confused-deputy patterns.

items.title: OAuth misconfiguration
items.description: Redirect URI validation, state parameter, and scope escalation. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Authentication and Authorization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Authentication and Authorization: Test "OAuth misconfiguration" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: API key exposure
items.description: Keys not in client bundles, logs, or referrer headers. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Authentication and Authorization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Authentication and Authorization: Test "API key exposure" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Mass assignment
items.description: Extra JSON fields like role:admin accepted by backend. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Input, Logic, and Operations. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Input, Logic, and Operations: Test "Mass assignment" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Rate limiting and brute force
items.description: OTP, login, and password reset endpoints throttled. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Input, Logic, and Operations. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: Input, Logic, and Operations: Test "Rate limiting and brute force" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: GraphQL introspection and depth limits
items.description: Disable introspection in prod; limit query depth and cost. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Input, Logic, and Operations. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Input, Logic, and Operations: Test "GraphQL introspection and depth limits" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Version sprawl
items.description: Old /api/v1 endpoints not left live with weaker auth. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Input, Logic, and Operations. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: Input, Logic, and Operations: Test "Version sprawl" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Error message leakage
items.description: Stack traces and internal IDs not returned to clients. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under Input, Logic, and Operations. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: Input, Logic, and Operations: Test "Error message leakage" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

sections.title: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA)

items.title: Tamper with sequential object identifiers
items.description: Increment, decrement, or substitute numeric object IDs in path and query parameters to access resources belonging to other users. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA): Test "Tamper with sequential object identifiers" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Access nested/child objects without ownership checks
items.description: Test child resources (e.g. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA). Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA): Test child resources (e.g
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Probe batch / bulk endpoints for object leakage
items.description: Submit arrays of IDs to batch endpoints to see whether per-object authorization is skipped for items the caller does not own. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA): Test "Probe batch / bulk endpoints for object leakage" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Assess predictability of UUID/GUID object references
items.description: Determine whether identifiers are guessable (sequential UUIDv1, timestamps, short tokens) so BOLA is exploitable without leaked IDs. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA): Test "Assess predictability of UUID/GUID object references" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Test BOLA on PATCH and DELETE operations
items.description: Verify state-changing verbs re-check ownership; read access may be guarded while modify/delete on the same object is not. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA): Verify state-changing verbs re-check ownership; read access may be guarded while modify/delete on the same object is not
items.how_to_fix: Enforce object-level authorization on every endpoint using server-side ownership checks - never trust client-supplied IDs alone.

items.title: Move object identifiers between path, query, and body
items.description: Relocate the object id into the request body or headers to bypass authorization logic that only inspects the URL path. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Level Authorization (BOLA): Test "Move object identifiers between path, query, and body" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

sections.title: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA)

items.title: Invoke administrative functions as a low-privilege user
items.description: Call admin-only endpoints (user management, config, reporting) with a standard user token to detect missing role enforcement. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA): Test "Invoke administrative functions as a low-privilege user" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Bypass authorization via alternate HTTP methods
items.description: Switch GET to POST/PUT/DELETE (or use overrides) to reach privileged behavior guarded only for the documented method. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA): Test "Bypass authorization via alternate HTTP methods" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Enforce object-level authorization on every endpoint using server-side ownership checks - never trust client-supplied IDs alone.

items.title: Enumerate endpoints lacking per-endpoint role checks
items.description: Map every route and confirm role/scope authorization is applied individually rather than relying on UI hiding or gateway-only rules. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA): Map every route and confirm role/scope authorization is applied individually rather than relying on UI hiding or gateway-only rules
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Discover hidden or guessable administrative paths
items.description: Brute-force admin namespaces (/admin, /internal, /v2/admin) that are unlinked but reachable without elevated authorization. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA): Test "Discover hidden or guessable administrative paths" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Tamper with role/scope claims in tokens or parameters
items.description: Modify role fields in JWT claims, cookies, or request parameters to escalate to privileged function access. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA): Test "Tamper with role/scope claims in tokens or parameters" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Cross-tenant privileged function access
items.description: Attempt privileged operations scoped to another tenant/organization to verify function authorization is tenant-aware. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Function Level Authorization (BFLA): Attempt privileged operations scoped to another tenant/organization to verify function authorization is tenant-aware
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

sections.title: API Access Control — Broken Object Property Level Authorization

items.title: Inject privileged properties via mass assignment
items.description: Add unexpected fields (isAdmin, role, balance, verified) to create/update bodies to test for blind binding to the data model. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Property Level Authorization: Add unexpected fields (isAdmin, role, balance, verified) to create/update bodies to test for blind binding to the data model
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Detect excessive data exposure in responses
items.description: Inspect responses for sensitive properties (PII, hashes, internal flags) the client should never receive but the API returns wholesale. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Property Level Authorization: Test "Detect excessive data exposure in responses" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Write to read-only or server-controlled properties
items.description: Attempt to modify fields meant to be immutable (created_at, owner_id, status) to verify property-level write authorization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Property Level Authorization: Attempt to modify fields meant to be immutable (created_at, owner_id, status) to verify property-level write authorization
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Bypass response field filtering
items.description: Use field-selection or include parameters (fields=, expand=, ?include) to coerce the API into returning restricted properties. Treat failures here as release blockers.

items.title: Set nested or relational privileged properties

items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Surface debug/internal properties in error responses
items.description: Trigger errors and edge cases to reveal internal property names or values that leak through verbose serialization. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Access Control — Broken Object Property Level Authorization: Test "Surface debug/internal properties in error responses" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Test weak or missing token validation
items.description: Submit malformed, expired, none-algorithm, or tampered tokens to confirm the server rigorously validates signature, expiry, and audience. Good hygiene that pays off over time.

items.title: Find endpoints missing authentication
items.description: Replay requests without credentials and brute-force routes to locate endpoints that respond without any authentication. Treat failures here as release blockers.

items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Assess credential stuffing and brute-force protections
items.description: Verify login and token endpoints enforce rate limiting, lockout, and anomaly detection against automated credential attacks. Treat failures here as release blockers.

items.title: Exploit JWT implementation flaws
items.description: Pointer to JWT-specific attacks: alg confusion, none algorithm, weak HMAC secrets, kid injection, and unverified jku/x5u. Good hygiene that pays off over time.

items.how_to_fix: Validate alg, iss, aud, and exp; use short-lived tokens; rotate signing keys; reject `none` and confused-deputy patterns.

items.title: Review the JWKS endpoint and key hygiene
items.description: The public JWKS (/.well-known/jwks.json) governs which keys verify tokens; stale, duplicate, or attacker-influencable keys widen the forgery surface and complicate revocation. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.

items.title: No pagination caps
items.description: Request unbounded result sets via limit/page-size parameters to confirm maximum result caps are enforced. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unrestricted Resource Consumption: Request unbounded result sets via limit/page-size parameters to confirm maximum result caps are enforced
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: File upload and multipart amplification
items.description: Upload large or many files (zip bombs, huge media) to test upload size limits and processing safeguards. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unrestricted Resource Consumption: Upload large or many files (zip bombs, huge media) to test upload size limits and processing safeguards
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Amplified third-party / billing cost abuse
items.description: Identify endpoints that trigger paid downstream calls (SMS, email, LLM) lacking quotas, causing financial denial of service. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unrestricted Resource Consumption: Test "Amplified third-party / billing cost abuse" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Rate-limit bypass via spoofed client headers
items.description: Per-IP throttles keyed on a forwarding header rather than the real connection can be reset by rotating the header value, defeating the limit entirely. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unrestricted Resource Consumption: Also test casing/param-pollution on the rate-limited key
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

sections.title: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery

items.title: URL parameters fetched server-side
items.description: Identify parameters (url, src, image, callback) the server requests and point them at internal or attacker-controlled hosts. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery: Test "URL parameters fetched server-side" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Webhook and callback URL abuse
items.description: Register webhook/callback URLs pointing at internal services to coerce server-side requests to private resources. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery: Test "Webhook and callback URL abuse" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Cloud metadata endpoint access
items.description: Target 169.254.169.254 (and equivalents) via SSRF to retrieve cloud instance credentials and metadata. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery: Test "Cloud metadata endpoint access" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: DNS rebinding to bypass host checks
items.description: Use TOCTOU DNS rebinding so a validated hostname resolves to an internal IP at fetch time. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery: Test "DNS rebinding to bypass host checks" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: SSRF allowlist/blocklist bypass
items.description: Bypass URL filters using IP encodings, redirects, alternate schemes, IPv6, and decimal/hex representations. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery: Test "SSRF allowlist/blocklist bypass" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Blind SSRF via out-of-band detection
items.description: Inject collaborator/OAST URLs to detect blind SSRF where no response content is returned. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Server-Side Request Forgery: Test "Blind SSRF via out-of-band detection" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

sections.title: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management

items.title: Old or deprecated API versions reachable
items.description: Probe /v1, /v0, and legacy paths that may lack current security controls but remain live. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management: Probe /v1, /v0, and legacy paths that may lack current security controls but remain live
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Undocumented / shadow APIs
items.description: Discover endpoints absent from documentation via brute force, JS analysis, and traffic inspection. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management: Test "Undocumented / shadow APIs" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Exposed non-production environments
items.description: Identify staging, dev, test, and debug hosts reachable from the internet that may hold weaker controls or real data. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management: Identify staging, dev, test, and debug hosts reachable from the internet that may hold weaker controls or real data
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Missing API version retirement
items.description: Verify a documented lifecycle exists so superseded versions are decommissioned rather than left running indefinitely. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management: Verify a documented lifecycle exists so superseded versions are decommissioned rather than left running indefinitely
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: API host and subdomain sprawl
items.description: Enumerate API subdomains and gateways to find forgotten hosts that escape monitoring and patching. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management: Test "API host and subdomain sprawl" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Documentation and spec drift
items.description: Compare OpenAPI/Swagger specs against live behavior to find endpoints/params present in one but not the other. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Improper Inventory Management: Test "Documentation and spec drift" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Missing security headers
items.description: Check for absent HSTS, CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, and cache-control headers on API responses. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration: Check for absent HSTS, CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, and cache-control headers on API responses
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Verbose error messages and stack traces
items.description: Trigger errors to reveal stack traces, framework versions, queries, or file paths leaking internal detail. Schedule remediation once higher-severity gaps are closed.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration: Test "Verbose error messages and stack traces" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Unnecessary HTTP methods enabled
items.description: Enumerate allowed methods (TRACE, OPTIONS, PUT, DELETE) and confirm dangerous or unused verbs are disabled. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration: Enumerate allowed methods (TRACE, OPTIONS, PUT, DELETE) and confirm dangerous or unused verbs are disabled
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Default configurations and credentials
items.description: Identify default admin paths, sample data, debug endpoints, and default credentials left from setup. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration: Test "Default configurations and credentials" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Weak transport security
items.description: Verify TLS configuration, certificate validity, and absence of cleartext API endpoints. Validate this when reviewing APIs, under API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration: Verify TLS configuration, certificate validity, and absence of cleartext API endpoints
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Overly permissive CORS policy
items.description: Check for wildcard origins or credentialed wildcard CORS that allows untrusted sites to call the API. Worth prioritising in your next assessment cycle.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Security Misconfiguration: Check for wildcard origins or credentialed wildcard CORS that allows untrusted sites to call the API
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

sections.title: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs

items.title: Test for blind trust of third-party API data
items.description: Data from integrated APIs consumed without validation can carry injection or malformed payloads downstream. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs: Test "Test for blind trust of third-party API data" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Verify transport security to integrated services
items.description: Calls to upstream APIs over plaintext or with disabled cert validation expose data to interception. Treat failures here as release blockers.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs: Test "Verify transport security to integrated services" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Test handling of redirects from third-party APIs
items.description: Blindly following redirects returned by an integrated API can enable SSRF or data exfiltration. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs: Test "Test handling of redirects from third-party APIs" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Trace consumed data into injection sinks
items.description: Third-party data reaching SQL/command/template/HTML sinks is exploitable just like direct user input. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs: Test "Trace consumed data into injection sinks" by swapping object IDs, tokens, and HTTP methods in Burp Repeater
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.

items.title: Test inbound webhook signature verification
items.description: Endpoints that receive provider webhooks (payment, VCS, CI) must verify the HMAC/asymmetric signature; missing or flawed verification lets an attacker forge state-changing events. Good hygiene that pays off over time.
items.how_to_find: API Resource & Posture — Unsafe Consumption of APIs: Endpoints that receive provider webhooks (payment, VCS, CI) must verify the HMAC/asymmetric signature; missing or flawed verification lets an attacker forge state-changing events
items.how_to_fix: Fix at the API gateway and service layer, add contract tests, and fuzz again with Burp or your CI DAST job.
